title: Identification + listing of hazardous waste
text: Two routes to RCRA hazardous: characteristic (D-codes: D001 ignitable, D002 corrosive, D003 reactive, D004-D043 toxicity by TCLP) and listed (F-, K-, P-, U-codes). F-list non-specific source; K-list specific source; P-list acute commercial chemical products; U-list non-acute commercial chemical products. Solid waste definition + exclusions in §261.4.

title: Generator standards + status
text: Three generator categories by monthly haz waste generation: VSQG ≤100 kg + ≤1 kg acute (P-list); SQG 100-1000 kg + ≤1 kg acute; LQG >1000 kg OR >1 kg acute. Re-categorize whenever monthly generation changes. EPA ID number required for SQG + LQG. Notification of Hazardous Waste Activity Form 8700-12 + biennial Form 8700-13A/B (LQG).

title: Accumulation time limits
text: LQG: 90 days at central accumulation area without permit. SQG: 180 days (270 if transport >200 miles). Satellite accumulation (≤55 gal or ≤1 qt acute) — no time limit until container fills; then 3 days to move to central. All containers marked 'Hazardous Waste' + accumulation start date + waste codes.

title: Hazardous waste manifest
text: Required for off-site shipment of hazardous waste. Tracks cradle-to-grave. e-Manifest system since 2018; paper still accepted with fees. Generator initiates; transporter + designated facility sign. Returned signed copy received within 35 days of shipment (45 SQG/LQG). Exception report if not received in 60 days (LQG). Retain 3 years.

title: Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR)
text: Hazardous waste must meet treatment standards (concentration-based UTS or technology-based) before land disposal. LDR notification accompanies each shipment to TSDF: waste codes, manifest #, treatment standard, certification of underlying constituents, generator info. Alternative treatment standard for soils + lab packs. Land Disposal Restriction Notification form.

title: TSDF standards
text: Standards for owners + operators of Treatment, Storage, Disposal Facilities. Part 264 permitted facilities (Part B); Part 265 interim status. Sections: preparedness + prevention; contingency plan + emergency procedures; personnel training; financial assurance; specific unit standards (containers 264 Subpart I, tanks J, surface impoundments K, landfills N, etc.).

title: Tank secondary containment
text: Tanks containing hazardous waste require secondary containment system designed + operated to: contain 100% of capacity of largest tank within containment, prevent run-on, drain accumulated liquids. Liner OR vault OR double-walled tank OR equivalent. Inspect weekly. Detect release within 24 hr.

title: Contingency plan + emergency coordinator
text: LQG + TSDF must prepare written contingency plan describing response to fires, explosions, releases. Coordinated with local police, fire department, hospitals, emergency response teams. Emergency coordinator on call 24/7. Plan kept on site + submitted to local LEPC + responders.

title: DOT hazmat training
text: Hazmat employees must receive general awareness, function-specific, safety, security awareness, in-depth security training. Recurrent every 3 years. Test + certification. Records retained 3 years past last training. Hazardous waste manifest signatory is a hazmat employee — DOT-trained.

title: Universal waste
text: Streamlined standards for: batteries, pesticides recalled/banned/unused, mercury-containing equipment (thermostats, switches), lamps (fluorescent + HID), aerosol cans. Small Quantity Handler (≤5000 kg total) + Large Quantity Handler. Accumulate up to 1 year; longer permissible only with documented justification. UW reduces generator status if managed properly.

title: Used oil
text: Used oil management standards (not RCRA hazardous unless mixed with hazardous waste). Generators, transporters, processors, marketers. Used oil filters managed separately (RCRA Subtitle D scrap metal under hot-drained exemption). On-spec vs. off-spec used oil burning rules. Mixed used oil + chlorinated solvent presumption.

title: Transporter standards
text: Hazardous waste transporters must: have EPA ID number, comply with manifest system, comply with DOT 49 CFR 171-180 HMR, immediately notify on discharge during transport, deliver only to designated TSDF on manifest. Joint EPA + DOT regulation. Hazmat Safety Permit (PHMSA) for certain Class 7 / Class 1.1 / poisonous-by-inhalation.
